title: Where all the hate comes from
display_title: Where all the hate comes from
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Where all the hate comes from ?             Quote Post by bladerj » Sun May 05, 2013 5:22 pm During the train wreck scene, there are a bunch of hate messages towards Shinji we didnt saw i the movie within Misato Asuka and gendo lines, some seem like coming from the crew but some seems to come from Asuka and mari. Anyone have the script, does it say wich character said what ?

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun May 05, 2013 5:24 pm ....What?

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Sun May 05, 2013 5:36 pm He talks after the revelation of Rei being his mother ?

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun May 05, 2013 5:38 pm airman4 wrote: He talks after the revelation of Rei being his mother ?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun May 05, 2013 6:00 pm There's some generic "hate messages" read by the female cast, some borrowed directly from the "ex-girlfriend montage" in EoE. I could recognize Yuko Miyamura, Megumi Hayashibara, and Kotono Mitsuishi, but maybe others are in there, too. Credit to UTW for actually discerning this stuff from the five audio channels. Even when you listen to them individually, some are nearly impossible to hear. 01:09:26 {Voice}　　　 What an idiot. I hate you. 01:09:27 {Voice}　　　 Stay away from me. 01:09:28 {Voice}　　　 To be honest, I don't just dislike you. 01:09:28 {Voice}　　　 I wish you didn't exist. 01:09:29 {Voice}　　　 There's no way I'd fall for you. 01:09:29 {Voice}　　　 Have a good think. 01:09:31 {Voice}　　　 I hate people like you the most. 01:09:31 {Voice}　　　 Just get lost. 01:09:43 {Voice}　　　 Your existence means nothing to me. 01:09:45 {Voice}　　　 Just disappear already. 01:09:47 {Voice}　　　 You'd burden me as a friend. 01:09:50 {Voice}　　　 To be honest, I don't just dislike you. 01:09:52 {Voice}　　　 I hate people like you the most. 01:09:55 {Voice}　　　 Bye-bye!

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun May 05, 2013 6:12 pm Mostly used for effect I'd argue. The lines that come directly from other characters in 3.0 are the most prevelant on the audio channels. The other hate lines are probably there to add a little bit more universiality to what Shinji is going through emotionally besides "Whether or not to pilot the 500 foot tall death machine" which a lot of people find is a decision they never have to make in their lifetime.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun May 05, 2013 6:23 pm The topic title is misleading. Gendo'sPapa wrote: Mostly used for effect I'd argue. The lines that come directly from other characters in 3.0 are the most prevelant on the audio channels. The other hate lines are probably there to add a little bit more universiality to what Shinji is going through emotionally besides "Whether or not to pilot the 500 foot tall death machine" which a lot of people find is a decision they never have to make in their lifetime.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun May 05, 2013 7:11 pm Reichu wrote: There's some generic "hate messages" read by the female cast, some borrowed directly from the "ex-girlfriend montage" in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by one-eyed » Sun May 05, 2013 7:25 pm I really felt at that moment in Shinji's skin. I could not imagine a more perfect hell for him, all your fears and nightmares come true. And what is more important, will become worse and worse and worse... to infinite. I do not wish this on anyone. It would be better to be dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sun May 05, 2013 7:32 pm one-eyed wrote: I really felt at that moment in Shinji's skin. I could not imagine a more perfect hell for him, all your fears and nightmares come true. And what is more important, will become worse and worse and worse... to infinite. I do not wish this on anyone. It would be better to be dead.

[]:             Quote Post by one-eyed » Sun May 05, 2013 7:36 pm Unfortunately, I agree with you.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon May 06, 2013 2:34 am ElMariachi wrote: What is this "ex-girlfriend montage" you're talking about?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon May 06, 2013 3:50 am Mr. Tines wrote: This bit, just before the live action section

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon May 06, 2013 6:57 am Stillborn wrote: That's why, no matter how I wish for Shinji to finally get some respect or at least happy ending, I see no way in hell that his mental collapse could be reversed in any believable way in 4.0. With emphasis on believable.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Mon May 06, 2013 7:04 am Who says Shinji will ever become accepted by the other characters? Perhaps he won't. Perhaps he'll die a meaningless death having learned nothing from his experiences. He would be far from the first main character in a film or series to meet that fate.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon May 06, 2013 7:17 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: Who says Shinji will ever become accepted by the other characters? Perhaps he won't. Perhaps he'll die a meaningless death having learned nothing from his experiences. He would be far from the first main character in a film or series to meet that fate.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Mon May 06, 2013 7:27 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: Who says Shinji will ever become accepted by the other characters? Perhaps he won't. Perhaps he'll die a meaningless death having learned nothing from his experiences. He would be far from the first main character in a film or series to meet that fate.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Mon May 06, 2013 10:59 am Maybe Shinji isn't actually responsible for the post-apocalyptic world, but Neo-Nerv and SEELE have wiped everyone's memories of what happened and brainwashed them into thinking Shinji did it? Gendo does seem to have predicted Misato's actions to some degree. Then, like Shinji remembering Yui's absorption into Eva-01 in Q, everyone regains their memories in Final (accompanied with psychadelic guilt trips?).

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Mon May 06, 2013 2:06 pm Stillborn wrote: You make it sound like he's to dumb to learn.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Wed May 08, 2013 6:55 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: Who says Shinji will ever become accepted by the other characters? Perhaps he won't. Perhaps he'll die a meaningless death having learned nothing from his experiences. He would be far from the first main character in a film or series to meet that fate.
